assertions.description: CSS.supports recognises the ruby-overhang property with its initial 'auto' value.
ruby-overhang accepts the 'spaces' keyword — annotations must not overlap adjacent boxes except over adjacent spaces.
Per CSSWG, 'none' is a legacy alias of 'spaces'; the parser accepts both and identical samples use the same layout width.
assertions.test: (()=>{const supports=(value)=>CSS.supports('ruby-overhang',value)||CSS.supports(`ruby-overhang: ${value}`);if(!supports('spaces')||!supports('none'))return false;const make=(value)=>{const span=document.createElement('span');span.style.cssText=`display:inline-block;font-size:24px;line-height:2.4;ruby-overhang:${value}`;span.innerHTML='前 <ruby>薔<rt>ばらのはな</rt></ruby> 後';document.body.appendChild(span);return span;};const spaces=make('spaces');const none=make('none');const delta=Math.abs(spaces.getBoundingClientRect().width-none.getBoundingClientRect().width);const s=(getComputedStyle(spaces).rubyOverhang||getComputedStyle(spaces).getPropertyValue('ruby-overhang')||spaces.style.getPropertyValue('ruby-overhang')||'').trim();const n=(getComputedStyle(none).rubyOverhang||getComputedStyle(none).getPropertyValue('ruby-overhang')||none.style.getPropertyValue('ruby-overhang')||'').trim();spaces.remove();none.remove();return delta<=1&&/^(spaces|none)$/.test(s)&&/^(spaces|none)$/.test(n);})()

assertions.description: An unrelated keyword like 'bananas' must not be reported as supported.
Setting rubyOverhang via CSSOM and reading getComputedStyle returns one of the keyword values.
The spec marks ruby-overhang as inherited. Setting it on a parent should propagate the computed value to a nested ruby annotation container.
